name: DefinitionsObserver
summary: This class is abstract. To implement this observer, create a Ruby class of this type, override the desired methods, and add an instance of the observer to the collection of interest. This observer interface is implemented to react to events on a definitions collection.

methods.name: onComponentAdded
methods.summary: The #onComponentAdded method is called whenever a definition is added to the definitions collection.
methods.remarks: The #onComponentAdded method is called whenever a definition is added to the definitions collection.

methods.name: onComponentPropertiesChanged
methods.summary: The #onComponentPropertiesChanged method is called whenever a definition's name or description are changed.
methods.remarks: The #onComponentPropertiesChanged method is called whenever a definition's name or description are changed. This does not fire when “Glue To”, “Cuts Opening”, or “Face Camera” settings are changed.

methods.name: onComponentRemoved
methods.summary: Note: This methods fires twice for each Component/Group erased.
methods.remarks: Note: This methods fires twice for each Component/Group erased. The #onComponentAdded method is called whenever a definition is removed from the definitions collection.

methods.name: onComponentTypeChanged
methods.summary: The #onComponentTypeChanged event is fired when a component is converted to a group or vice versa.
methods.remarks: The #onComponentTypeChanged event is fired when a component is converted to a group or vice versa. (In the underlying implementation, Groups are just a special kind of definition that is allowed to only have a single instance.)
